findings.title: North Star Metric: Search Relevance Score
findings.description: The North Star metric for SearchBoost is the Search Relevance Score, which measures the effectiveness of search results as perceived by users. This metric is critical for understanding user satisfaction and engagement with search results.
assumptions: Search relevance directly correlates with user satisfaction and engagement.
recommendations: Monitor search relevance scores closely and investigate factors contributing to the drop.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Search Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The Search Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of search results that users click on after performing a search. This is a key indicator of the effectiveness of search results in attracting user engagement.
assumptions: Higher CTR indicates better search result relevance.
recommendations: Investigate the factors leading to the decline in CTR and optimize search results.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Session Abandonment After Search
findings.description: This metric tracks the percentage of sessions that are abandoned after a search is performed. A high abandonment rate may indicate dissatisfaction with search results.
assumptions: Lower session abandonment rates correlate with higher user satisfaction.
recommendations: Implement measures to reduce session abandonment rates.

findings.title: Instrumentation Gap for New Query Types
findings.description: There is a significant gap in data collection for new query types introduced in v2.4.1, specifically image search and voice search. Without instrumentation for these query types, we lack visibility into their performance and user engagement.
assumptions: Instrumentation is necessary for measuring the success of new features.

evidence.excerpt: No analytics events were instrumented for these new query types.

recommendations: Implement baseline instrumentation for image search and voice search to enable future analysis.

findings.title: Establish Baseline Metrics for Image Search
findings.description: Baseline metrics for image search need to be established to measure its performance effectively. This includes query volume, zero-result rate, and click-through rate.
assumptions: Establishing baselines is crucial for measuring improvements.

evidence.excerpt: We need to establish: query volume, zero-result rate, click-through rate on image results.

recommendations: Conduct an audit to implement baseline metrics for image search.

summary: The analysis identified a critical drop in search relevance scores and associated metrics, indicating potential user dissatisfaction. Key metrics have been defined, including a North Star metric, primary KPIs, and guardrail metrics. Significant gaps in instrumentation for new query types were also highlighted, necessitating immediate action to establish baselines and improve data collection.
